name: Crock- Pot Pork Chops & Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 small cabbage, cut into thin wedges
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove any excess fat from the pork chops. Season each chop with 2 tablespoons of flour, shaking off any excess.
2. Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat. Brown the pork chops for about 2-3 minutes on each side until golden brown. Transfer to a plate.
3. In the slow cooker, combine potatoes, onion, and carrots. Sprinkle the remaining flour evenly over the vegetables and stir to coat.
4. Layer cabbage, salt, pepper, browned pork chops, beef bouillon granules, 1/4 cup water, and stewed tomatoes with juice on top of the vegetable mixture in the slow cooker. Do not stir.
5. Cover and cook on high for 3 1/2 to 4 hours or low for 7-8 hours until the pork is cooked through.
6. (Optional) In a small saucepan, mix sour cream and cornstarch until smooth. Gradually add water or milk to reach a total of 1 1/2 cups. Stir in the beef bouillon mixture until well combined.
6. Cook over medium heat until thickened and bubbly. Serve the sauce alongside the pork chops.
